text: We tested general multimodal image models with explicit prompts, UV explanations, and reference images.
pronounce_text: We tested general multimodal image models with explicit prompts, UV explanations, and reference images.

text: The request sounds reasonable: generate a 64 by 64 Minecraft skin UV map for this character.
pronounce_text: The request sounds reasonable: generate a sixty-four by sixty-four Minecraft skin UV map for this character.

text: But a usable skin is not judged by style alone. Every visible pixel has to snap to the exact 64 by 64 UV grid.
pronounce_text: But a usable skin is not judged by style alone. Every visible pixel has to snap to the exact sixty-four by sixty-four UV grid.

text: The first two outputs look skin-like at a glance, but their pixels are not strictly aligned to the Minecraft layout.
pronounce_text: The first two outputs look skin-like at a glance, but their pixels are not strictly aligned to the Minecraft layout.

text: They may invent regions, shift faces, blur structural boundaries, ignore the inner and outer layer relationship, or fail to output transparency in a usable way.
pronounce_text: They may invent regions, shift faces, blur structural boundaries, ignore the inner and outer layer relationship, or fail to output transparency in a usable way.

text: So the problem is not that these models cannot make attractive images. They can. The problem is that they do not consistently obey the exact schema of a Minecraft skin.
pronounce_text: So the problem is not that these models cannot make attractive images. They can. The problem is that they do not consistently obey the exact schema of a Minecraft skin.

text: For a normal image, approximate structure might be acceptable. For a game texture with strict UV coordinates, it breaks the asset.
pronounce_text: For a normal image, approximate structure might be acceptable. For a game texture with strict UV coordinates, it breaks the asset.
